skill.name: rag-implementation
skill.description: Build Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems for LLM applications using vector databases, semantic search, chunking, reranking, and grounded prompts.
skill.summary: Design RAG pipelines that retrieve source context and produce grounded LLM answers.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown documentation examples rather than executable skill code. The Ruby backtick findings are false positives caused by fenced Python code blocks. The remaining concerns are low-risk instructional examples that mention a placeholder API key and a localhost vector database URL.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many Ruby or shell backtick executions, but the cited regions are fenced Python examples in SKILL.md. No executable script, shell invocation, or command injection path was found in the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visibly Markdown fenced code examples. The skill contains no separate executable file or instruction to run hidden shell commands.

low_findings.title: Placeholder API Key in Documentation Example
low_findings.description: The Pinecone example includes api_key="your-api-key" as a placeholder. This is not credential theft or environment access, but users should replace it with secure secret handling when implementing the example.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The value is an obvious placeholder inside a documentation snippet. The risk is limited to copy-paste security hygiene rather than malicious skill behavior.

low_findings.title: Localhost Network URL in Vector Store Example
low_findings.description: The Weaviate example connects to http://localhost:8080. This is a local service configuration example, not an external exfiltration endpoint or hidden network call.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL points to localhost and appears in a clearly labeled Weaviate configuration example. No evidence of data exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Terms Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance blocker alerts map to ordinary RAG terminology and retrieval strategy text. No cryptographic operation, system probing, prompt injection attempt, or privilege escalation instruction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found domain vocabulary and headings only. There is no semantic evidence of cryptographic misuse or reconnaissance behavior.

content.value_statement: LLM applications often need reliable answers from private or changing knowledge sources. This skill guides RAG design with chunking, embeddings, retrieval, reranking, prompting, and evaluation.
actual_capabilities: Explains core RAG components, including vector stores, embeddings, retrieval strategies, and reranking.
Shows common implementation patterns using LangChain-style document loading, chunking, retrieval, and QA chains.
Compares retrieval approaches such as dense search, sparse search, hybrid search, multi-query retrieval, and HyDE.
Provides chunking strategies for recursive text, token-based text, semantic chunks, and Markdown headers.
Covers prompt patterns for grounded answers, citations, and confidence scoring.
Outlines evaluation metrics for answer accuracy, retrieval quality, and groundedness.
limitations: The skill is instructional documentation, not a maintained runnable package.
Code examples may require dependency updates for current LangChain or provider SDK versions.
External services such as OpenAI, Pinecone, Cohere, or Weaviate require separate accounts and credentials.
It does not provide production security controls for sensitive document ingestion or access control.

use_cases.title: Document Q&A Assistant
use_cases.description: Build a system that answers questions from internal manuals, support articles, or policy documents with source context.

use_cases.title: Research Knowledge Tool
use_cases.description: Create a retrieval workflow that searches large research collections and returns grounded summaries with citations.

use_cases.title: Enterprise Search Prototype
use_cases.description: Compare vector database options, chunking methods, and reranking strategies before committing to a production RAG design.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic RAG Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rag-implementation skill to design a basic RAG pipeline for my document Q&A app. Include ingestion, chunking, embeddings, retrieval, prompting, and evaluation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Retrieval Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rag-implementation skill to compare dense, sparse, hybrid, and multi-query retrieval for my use case. Recommend one approach and explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Retrieval Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rag-implementation skill to diagnose poor retrieval quality in my RAG system. Focus on chunk size, overlap, metadata filters, reranking, and evaluation metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Production RAG Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rag-implementation skill to review my production RAG architecture. Check retrieval strategy, vector store design, prompt grounding, citations, monitoring, and failure modes.

output_examples.input: Plan a RAG system for a support knowledge base.
output: Recommended pipeline: load support articles, split by headings, embed chunks, store vectors with product metadata, retrieve top results, rerank, and answer with citations.
Evaluation should include representative support questions, expected articles, answer accuracy, and groundedness checks.

output_examples.input: Help me decide between Chroma, Pinecone, and Weaviate.
output: Chroma fits local prototypes, Pinecone fits managed production search, and Weaviate fits hybrid search with open-source deployment options.
The final choice depends on scale, operations ownership, filtering needs, and compliance requirements.

output_examples.input: My RAG answers include irrelevant sources.
output: Likely fixes include smaller chunks, better metadata filters, hybrid retrieval, cross-encoder reranking, and test cases that measure retrieval quality separately from answer quality.
Start by inspecting the retrieved documents before changing the generation prompt.

best_practices: Start with a small evaluation set before tuning chunk sizes, retrieval depth, or reranking.
Return source documents or citations so users can verify grounded answers.
Track retrieval quality separately from final answer quality during testing.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on a larger prompt alone when retrieval returns weak context.
Do not index sensitive documents without access controls and retention rules.
Do not tune only on happy-path questions that already match document titles.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps design RAG systems that retrieve relevant documents before generating grounded LLM answers.

faq.question: Does it include runnable project files?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation skill with examples and design guidance, not a complete application template.

faq.question: Which vector databases does it cover?
faq.answer: It discusses Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus, Chroma, Qdrant, and FAISS at a selection and configuration level.

faq.question: Can it reduce hallucinations?
faq.answer: Yes, it explains grounding prompts, source citations, retrieval quality checks, and evaluation methods that reduce unsupported answers.

faq.question: Does it handle private data security?
faq.answer: Only at a high level. Production systems still need access control, secret management, audit logs, and data retention policies.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is written for AI coding assistants that can apply RAG design guidance to application code.
